# 00 — Scope

**Audited surface:** the print/export documents generated by `js/export.js`, focused on
Ethics Committee correspondence issued "on behalf of the SCP Foundation":

| Document | Builder | Kind |
|---|---|---|
| Invitation to Interview | `buildInterviewInviteHTML(r, actor, false)` | candidate-facing letter |
| Notice of Appointment | `buildInterviewInviteHTML(r, actor, true)` | candidate-facing letter |
| Interview Feedback sheet | `buildFeedbackSheetHTML` | candidate-facing letter |
| Interviewer's Script | `buildInterviewScriptHTML` | internal instrument |
| Summons to Appear | `buildSummonsHTML` | formal instrument |
| Record of Proceedings | `buildCaseDocumentHTML` | formal record |

Shared chrome: `frameDoc`, `letterhead`, `signBlock`, `CSS` const, `IV_STYLE` (js/export.js).

**Primary user:** an SCP-roleplay community member receiving/serving these as in-fiction
official Foundation paperwork (printed or PDF). **Primary task:** read a formal document
that credibly looks issued by the Ethics Committee of the SCP Foundation.

**Constraints:** self-contained HTML (no external assets), print-first (A4), existing
org seals (logos.js), house classification bars must stay, no build step.

**Verdict rules note:** user explicitly wants a professionalisation pass — but per the
skill, the verdict follows the scorecard, not the preference.
